name: checksec
title: checksec
summary: Bash script to test executable properties
description:
Bash script to test executable properties
Modern Linux distributions offer some mitigation techniques to make it harder
to exploit software vulnerabilities reliably. Mitigations such as RELRO,
NoExecute (NX), Stack Canaries, Address Space Layout Randomization (ASLR) and
Position Independent Executables (PIE) have made reliably exploiting any
vulnerabilities that do exist far more challenging. The checksec.sh script is
designed to test what standard Linux OS and PaX security features are being
used.

arguments.description: Checks the security features of all running processes
arguments.raw: --proc-all  Checks the security features of all running processes

arguments.description: Checks the security features of the all libraries of a running process ID
arguments.raw: --proc-libs  Checks the security features of the all libraries of a running process ID

arguments.description: Shows the current version of the running software
arguments.raw: --version  Shows the current version of the running software

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use checksec because Bash script to test executable properties.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use checksec as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `checksec [options] [file]` as documented upstream for checksec.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair checksec with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `checksec --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
